what_it_does: Defines an SVG left-facing chevron icon as a stroked path within a 24 by 24 viewBox.
observations: The root svg uses the SVG namespace and viewBox "0 0 24 24".
The icon has no fill and uses currentColor for its stroke.
Stroke width is 1.5 with round line caps and round line joins.
A single path draws points from 15,6 to 9,12 to 15,18.
standouts: The stroke inherits color from the surrounding context via currentColor.
The geometry is a single open polyline forming a left-facing chevron.
conventions: Uses stroke-only line icon styling with rounded endpoints and joins.
Keeps the SVG compact with inline attributes and one path element.
concerns: No title, role, or aria metadata is present in the SVG itself.
notes: This file is a compact, color-inheriting SVG asset for a left chevron icon.
questions: Should accessibility labeling be supplied by the consuming component?
purpose_vs_actual: The filename indicates a left chevron, and the SVG path draws a left-facing chevron.
intention_signals: It appears intended to provide a reusable left-chevron navigation icon that adapts to surrounding text or icon color.
contribution: Contributes a compact left-chevron SVG glyph with inherited stroke color and rounded line styling for use in a fused icon asset set.
summary_for_embedding: A 24 by 24 SVG left-chevron icon made from one stroked path using currentColor, no fill, 1.5 stroke width, and rounded caps and joins.
intention_for_embedding: This was for representing a leftward chevron or back/navigation affordance as a reusable color-inheriting SVG icon.
